title: Duplicate function definition in same file or across loaded modules
description: Defining the same function name more than once causes silent override. The last definition wins, but earlier definitions may have been intended for different code paths. In modules, this can cause unpredictable behaviour depending on import order. Already checked by SyntaxGuard (check #6) but this SIN tracks the full cross-module scope.
detection_method: AST scan: parse all .ps1/.psm1 files, extract function definitions, flag duplicates within same file and across module boundary
preventionRule: Each function name must be unique across the loaded module set. If override is intentional, add a # SIN-EXEMPT: Function override intentional comment.
scan_logic: Extract all function names via regex, group by name, flag groups with count > 1
remedy: Rename the duplicate or consolidate into a single implementation. If both are needed, namespace with module prefix (e.g. ModuleName-FunctionName).
